title: Paridis rhizoma total saponins ameliorate abnormal uterine bleeding via regulating Proteus mirabilis 2024-mediated estrogen metabolism in the gut-reproductive axis.
publicationName: Phytomedicine : international journal of phytotherapy and phytopharmacology
journalName: Phytomedicine : international journal of phytotherapy and phytopharmacology
snippet: Abnormal uterine bleeding (AUB) is a highly prevalent gynecological disorder worldwide, for which first-line steroid hormone therapy is limited by well-documented adverse effects. Paridis rhizoma total saponins (PRS) are the core pharmacodynamic material basis of Gongxuening Capsules, a classic traditional Chinese medicine (TCM) formula with well-validated....
